title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) Vulnerability
description: The system’s handling of user-supplied data, particularly within web pages, is not properly sanitized. This allows an attacker to inject malicious JavaScript code into a website, which can then be executed by other users’ browsers, potentially stealing cookies, redirecting users to malicious sites, or defacing the website.
attack_vector: Injection of malicious JavaScript code into input fields or URLs.
mitigations: Implement output encoding to properly escape special characters before displaying data in web pages.
Use a Content Security Policy (CSP) to restrict the sources from which the browser can load resources.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter out malicious requests.
